name: Deep Book vs Trade Flow Divergence
hypothesis: A strong predictive signal emerges when the patient, deep-book liquidity and the immediate realized trade flow are in opposition. Specifically, if the deep book is bid-heavy (obi_ex_bbo > 0.3) suggesting underlying support, but recent trade flow has been strongly negative (zscore < -2.0), it may indicate a 'bear trap' or absorption event where sellers are exhausted into a wall of buyers. This divergence should precede a short-term price rebound.
mutation_note: New spec exploring the interaction between two orthogonal Block C primitives. Inspired by the success of 'bid wall absorption' signals from prior iterations (e.g., iter006_bid_wall_absorption_tight), but using the more stable deep-book imbalance (obi_ex_bbo) instead of the noisy BBO (obi_1).
